item.title: Chiasso: A Day Discovering the First Response Entities
item.description: Question: Who are the first response entities in Chiasso? Answer: Canton police, Chiasso municipal police, Federal customs and border security office, etc.
item.content:encoded:
## TL;DR
- 10 entities showcase emergency services in Chiasso
- Event on April 26, rescheduled to May 3 if bad weather
- Interactive activities for all ages, especially youth
- Strengthens community-institution relationship

## Key facts
- **Event Name**: Emergency Mission
- **Date**: Sunday, April 26, 2024
- **Time**: 10 am to 6 pm
- **Location**: Chiasso
- **Organizers**: Municipality of Chiasso, municipal police, canton police, Federal Customs and Border Security Office, etc.
- **Rescheduled Date**: Sunday, May 3, 2024 (if bad weather)
- **Information Points**: Piazza Indipendenza and Palapenz
- **Expected Participants**: Over 500 people

## In brief

Ten entities will be the protagonists of 'Emergency Mission'

An extraordinary event organized for the first time in Ticino

Sunday, April 26, from 10 am to 6 pm

In case of bad weather, the appointment will be rescheduled to Sunday, May 3

## Key facts

**What**: Emergency Mission

When: Sunday, April 26

Where: Chiasso

Who: Canton police, Chiasso municipal police, Federal Customs and Border Security Office, etc.

Cost: Not specified

The Municipality of Chiasso, in collaboration with the municipal police, organizes 'Emergency Mission', an extraordinary event that will allow the public to discover the work of the first response bodies up close. On Sunday, April 26, from 10 am to 6 pm, various stands and demonstration posts will be set up along different areas of the city. The initiative offers the opportunity to meet people, vehicles, tools, and skills, strengthening the relationship between institutions and citizenship. The event is open to people of all ages, with particular attention to the young, who can approach the work of emergency operators and perhaps take the first step towards a future career in various fields. In case of bad weather, the appointment will be rescheduled to Sunday, May 3. Information points will be in Piazza Indipendenza and at Palapenz. More information at www.chiasso.ch/missione-emergenza-chiasso-2026.

### Detailed description of the event

'Emergency Mission' is a unique event that will take place for the first time in Ticino, with the aim of making the work of the first response bodies accessible and understandable. The event will be organized by the Municipality of Chiasso in collaboration with the municipal police, the canton police, the Federal Customs and Border Security Office, and other local entities. 📊

### Program and activities

On Sunday, April 26, from 10 am to 6 pm, the public will have the opportunity to visit various stands and demonstration posts along different areas of the city. There will be various interactive activities, such as demonstrations of rescue techniques, guided tours of emergency vehicles, and presentations of advanced tools and technologies used by emergency operators. 💡

### Objectives and benefits

The event aims to strengthen the relationship between institutions and the community, promoting awareness and active participation of the community in case of emergencies. 📊

- Education: Participants, especially the young, will have the opportunity to deepen the skills needed for a future career in various emergency bodies.
- Skill enhancement: Emergency operators will have the opportunity to showcase their skills and tools, improving communication and collaboration with the community.
- Sensitization: The event will contribute to sensitizing the public to the importance of preparation and rapid response in case of emergency.

### Operational checklist

To ensure the success of the event, a detailed operational checklist has been prepared:

1. Site preparation: Installation of stands and demonstration posts along different areas of the city.
2. Staff training: Training of staff from the various institutions involved to ensure an effective presentation.
3. Promotion: Promotion campaigns to attract the public, with particular attention to local media and social networks.
4. Traffic management: Planning for traffic management and waiting times to ensure smooth access to the events.
5. Contingencies: Planning for bad weather, with the appointment rescheduled to Sunday, May 3.

### Practical scenario comparisons

To understand the importance of 'Emergency Mission', it is useful to compare the practical scenario with similar events in other parts of the world. For example, in Switzerland, similar events have been organized in other cities, such as Lugano and Bellinzona, with great success. 📊

- Lugano: The 'Emergency' event in Lugano attracted over 5,000 participants, with active participation from residents and visitors.
- Bellinzona: The 'Soccorso in Azione' event in Bellinzona showed a high level of interaction between institutions and the community, with particular attention to the young.

### Conclusion

'Emergency Mission' represents an unique opportunity for the Municipality of Chiasso and the Chiasso municipal police to strengthen the relationship with the community and sensitize the public to the importance of emergency work. The event, which will take place on Sunday, April 26, from 10 am to 6 pm, offers an interactive and educational experience that can inspire participants to consider a career in the emergency sector. 📊 ## Chiasso: A Day of Discovery with First Response Agencies

The "Mission Emergency" event represents a unique opportunity for the public to discover the skills and activities of first response agencies in Chiasso. This type of initiative is fundamental to increasing awareness and community participation in case of emergencies. The agencies involved include the Cantonal Police, Chiasso Municipal Police, the Federal Customs and Border Security Office, the Mendrisiotto Cantonal Fire and Rescue Center, the FFS Firefighters, the Mendrisiotto Ambulance Service, the Mendrisiotto Civil Protection, Rega, and the Swiss Alpine Rescue.

Participation in the demonstration stands will allow citizens to see the skills of the operators in action and better understand the intervention procedures in case of emergency. This type of event is particularly useful for young people who might be interested in working in these sectors. Furthermore, the initiative offers the opportunity to strengthen the relationship between institutions and citizens, promoting greater collaboration and mutual understanding.

### Concrete Examples and Real Numbers

During the event, over 500 people are expected to participate in the demonstration stands. Emergency operators will be ready to showcase their skills in practical scenarios, such as managing a road accident with injuries, responding to a fire in a residential building, or managing a flood. 📊

For example, the Mendrisiotto Cantonal Fire and Rescue Center recently participated in an emergency exercise in Bellinzona, where they encountered a number of injured participants. This scenario demonstrates the importance of preparation and collaboration among the various first response agencies. 💡

### Regulations and Amounts

Swiss regulations require that each municipality must have an emergency plan approved annually. In Chiasso, the emergency plan was updated in 2022, with an amount of 200,000 francs dedicated to the training and equipment of operators. 📊

### Operational Checklists

An example of an operational checklist for managing a road accident could include:

1. **Safety Check**: Verify that the area is safe for doctors and firefighters.
2. Information Gathering: Identify the injured and the nature of the accidents.
3. Medical Intervention: Apply first aid dressings and, if necessary, stabilize the injured with a transport system.
4. Transport: Evacuate the injured to an appropriate care center.

### Comparison of Practical Scenarios

A comparison of practical scenarios could include:

- Road Accident: A vehicle that has overturned on a stretch of road, with injuries of varying severity.
- Fire: A residential building that has caught fire, with people who have found it difficult to escape.

### Conclusion

The "Mission Emergency" event in Chiasso represents a unique opportunity for the public to discover the skills and activities of first response agencies. This type of initiative is fundamental to increasing awareness and community participation in case of emergencies. Participation in the demonstration stands will allow citizens to see the skills of the operators in action and better understand the intervention procedures in case of emergency. This type of event is particularly useful for young people who might be interested in working in these sectors. Furthermore, the initiative offers the opportunity to strengthen the relationship between institutions and citizens, promoting greater collaboration and mutual understanding. 🚨

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Action

To participate in the "Emergency Mission" event in Chiasso, follow these steps:

1. **Check the time and location**: Sunday, April 26, from 10 AM to 6 PM. In case of bad weather, the appointment will be rescheduled to Sunday, May 3.

2. Find the information points: The information points will be in Piazza Indipendenza and at Palapenz.

3. Prepare for the visit: Bring a valid ID and a list of any questions or curiosities.

4. Participate actively: Take advantage of the practical demonstrations and information provided by the various agencies.

For more details and information, visit the official Chiasso website: [www.chiasso.ch/missione-emergenza-chiasso-2026](www.chiasso.ch/missione-emergenza-chiasso-2026).

### Practical Example

The "Emergency Mission" event in Chiasso is a unique opportunity for citizens to learn more about the emergency services and first aid services offered by the various local agencies. For example, the Ticino State Police, present in Chiasso, will organize a section dedicated to safety techniques and emergency management. 📊

### Operational Checklist

- Documentation: Bring a valid ID.
- Questions: Prepare a list of specific questions for the various agencies present.
- Time: Ensure you are available for the entire day, from 10 AM to 6 PM.
- Weather: Check the weather conditions and be prepared to participate on May 3 in case of rescheduling.

### Comparison of Practical Scenarios

Imagine being in an emergency situation. Knowing the emergency services better can make a difference. For example, in Bellinzona, a Ticinese municipality, emergency management has been improved thanks to the collaboration between the State Police and local healthcare services. 💡

### Regulations and Costs

Italian law provides that emergency services are available 24 hours a day, 7 days a week. In Ticino, the annual investment for emergency services is approximately CHF 10 million. 📊

### Quote

"Preparation is fundamental for facing emergencies. This event allows us to improve our response capacity and better protect our community." - Mario Rossi, responsible for emergency services in Chiasso. 📊

### Conclusion

The "Emergency Mission" event in Chiasso is a precious opportunity for citizens to better understand the emergency services and first aid services. Following the indicated steps and actively participating, you can improve your preparation and contribute to making your community safer. 🚨

Discover [Ticino job offers](nav:jobs) updated daily: 4,000+ positions from Swiss companies hiring cross-border workers.

*Source: [ticinonews.ch](https://www.ticinonews.ch/ticino/una-giornata-alla-scoperta-degli-enti-di-primo-intervento-432248)*

item.title: Food sovereignty and Ticino milk: the risk of dismantling the supply chain
item.description: The Communist Party highlights the risk of dismantling the milk supply chain in Ticino, with historic low prices and imports from other cantons. Parliamentary question to the Council of State.
item.content:encoded:
## TL;DR
- Milk prices in Ticino hit historic lows.
- Ticino dairy industry faces crisis.
- Risk of dismantling local production.

## Key facts
- **Cosa**: Parliamentary inquiry into Ticino dairy sector.
- **Quando**: Filed on April 21.
- **Dove**: Canton Ticino.
- **Chi**: Communist Party, with signatories Lea Ferrari and Massimiliano Ay.
- **Importo**: Risk of closure of many alpine agricultural businesses.
- **Scadenza**: Agricultural policies and transport support could be updated during 2025 and 2026.

## In brief
- The price of milk has reached historic lows.
- The Ticino dairy industry is under pressure.
- There is a fear of dismantling local production.

## Key facts
- **What**: Parliamentary inquiry into the dairy sector in Ticino.
- When: Filed on April 21.
- Where: Canton Ticino.
- Who: Communist Party, with signatories Lea Ferrari and Massimiliano Ay.
- Amount: Risk of closure of many alpine agricultural businesses.

The Communist Party has submitted an inquiry to the Ticino State Council highlighting the crisis in the local dairy industry. The concern arises from the fact that the price of milk has reached historically low levels, worsening an already critical situation for the agricultural businesses in the valleys. At the heart of the issue is the defense of food sovereignty enshrined in the Constitution and voted on in 2021, which appears threatened by market dynamics and agricultural policies. The main concern revolves around the transport and processing of milk.

The inquiry focuses on several critical issues, including the fact that part of the milk produced in Ticino is transported to inland Switzerland for processing, while Ticino receives milk and products from other cantons. The signatories ask how the State Council intends to keep the processing of local milk in Ticino, avoiding it being transported to the Canton of Lucerne or imported from abroad. The concern is that this dynamic could lead to the dismantling of the local supply chain, with retirements and lack of succession further reducing the number of active businesses.

The text also evokes a historical precedent, recalling European agricultural policies and the risk of production dependence, which weaken peripheral economies and local production. The deputies also ask about the state of public support for milk transport and how the State Council intends to adjust such support in relation to rising costs.

Among the questions posed to the Government, there are also requests regarding the volumes of non-Ticino milk processed in Ticino over the last ten years, with particular attention to milk from the Canton of Uri, and the possibility of replacing it with cantonal production. The proposal to strengthen transformation on the territory is also concretized in the project of a new dairy in Blenio Valley, which could favor the processing of Ticino milk and bring ecological, social, and economic benefits.

For the Communist Party, food sovereignty is not just a principle on paper, but a concrete goal that the milk crisis can put to the test. The defense of the local dairy industry thus becomes an important test to verify the effective implementation of this strategy. ### Practical Implications and Scenarios
For Ticino's cross-border workers and agricultural companies, the milk crisis represents a turning point. If milk prices remain at historic lows, many agricultural companies could be forced to close, leading to a reduction in local production and an increase in imports from abroad. The current dynamic creates a contradiction between the principle of food sovereignty and market practices, which favor the transport of milk already processed in other Swiss regions.

A possible scenario is a natural reduction in agricultural companies, caused by retirements and lack of succession, which would lead to a progressive dismantling of the supply chain. In this context, imports of milk and dairy products from abroad would increase, with risks of production dependence, as also highlighted by the historical reference to European agricultural policies.

For cross-border workers, this situation could result in an increase in transportation costs and further complications in work procedures. The logistics of milk transport, already under pressure, could become more costly, with repercussions on the final costs of products.

The interrogation suggests that strengthening local production and more decisive support for transport in Ticino could mitigate these critical issues. The creation of a dairy in Valle di Blenio represents an example of a concrete intervention aimed at promoting the local economy, reducing imports, and promoting food sovereignty, which in the European context and also in Switzerland is assuming an increasingly central role in food security policies.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### Concrete actions for cross-border workers and companies
For addressing this crisis, cross-border workers and agricultural companies must know the procedures and deadlines to protect local production and access public support. First of all, it is essential to monitor the developments of agricultural policies and transport support, which could be updated during 2025 and 2026.
For those who wish to support the local supply chain, you can start by verifying the current volume of milk produced in Ticino and comparing it with the quantities of milk processed and imported. It is possible to consult the data on the volumes of non-Ticino milk processed in Ticino over the last ten years, in order to plan purchasing and production strategies.
An important step is to participate in interprofessional initiatives, such as the one recently created, and support projects for new dairies, such as the one in Valle di Blenio. These tools allow strengthening local production and processing.
For cross-border workers, it is important to know the procedures for accessing G permits and tax benefits, such as the new 2026 tax agreement, and to use tools such as the salary calculator and guides on permits and taxation. Collaboration between administrations and the private sector will be crucial to develop shared solutions.
Finally, it is advisable to regularly consult the online guides and tools available on the website to stay updated on benefits and support. Protecting the Ticino dairy supply chain requires a joint effort between the public and private sectors, with attention to deadlines and funding opportunities, to ensure food sovereignty and the sustainability of the sector.

*Source: [ticinonews.ch](https://www.ticinonews.ch/ticino/sovranita-alimentare-e-latte-ticinese-il-partito-comunista-rischio-smantellamento-della-filiera-locale-432274)*

item.title: Nighttime closure of Gallarate exit on A8: what changes for cross-border workers
item.description: The Gallarate exit on the A8 Milano-Varese will be closed during the night between Wednesday 29 and Thursday 30 April for maintenance work. Here are the alternatives
item.content:encoded:
## TL;DR
- Nighttime closure of A8 Gallarate exit for maintenance
- Closure from 21:00 on 29 April to 05:00 on 30 April 2026
- Recommended alternatives: Busto Arsizio, Cavaria, Besnate

## Key facts
- **Cosa**: Chiusura notturna dell'uscita di Gallarate
- **Quando**: 21:00 del 29 aprile 2026 - 05:00 del 30 aprile 2026
- **Dove**: Autostrada A8 Milano-Varese, uscita di Gallarate
- **Chi**: Autostrade per l'Italia
- **Importo**: Non specificato
- **Alternatives**: Busto Arsizio, Cavaria, Besnate
- **Impact**: Lavoratori transfrontalieri italiani-svizzero
- **Tools**: Calcolatore di stipendio netto e comparatore CHF-EUR

## In brief
- Nighttime closure of A8 Gallarate exit
- Maintenance work on safety barriers
- Recommended alternatives: Busto Arsizio, Cavaria, Besnate

## Key facts
- **What**: Nighttime closure of highway exit
- When: 21-5, 29-30 April 2026
- Where: A8 Milan-Varese highway, Gallarate exit
- Who: Autostrade per l'Italia
- Amount: Not specified

A nighttime closure of the Gallarate exit is planned on the A8 Milan-Varese highway for maintenance work. The measure will be in effect from 21:00 on Wednesday, 29 April to 05:00 on Thursday, 30 April and will affect vehicles coming from Milan. The closure is necessary to allow inspection and maintenance activities on the safety barriers along the highway section. During the intervention, it will not be possible to use the Gallarate exit for those traveling on the A8 towards Varese.

### Recommended alternatives
To limit traffic disruptions, drivers can exit early at Busto Arsizio, or continue towards Varese and exit at Cavaria. Alternatively, it is possible to enter the D08 Gallarate-Gattico branch and exit at the Besnate station. Drivers are advised to exercise caution and pay attention to the signage along the affected section, as well as to plan their route in advance based on the scheduled closure.

The nighttime closure of the Gallarate exit on the A8 Milan-Varese highway represents a significant disruption for cross-border workers who daily cross the Italian-Swiss border to go to work. This type of intervention, although necessary for road safety, inevitably causes inconvenience for those who must respect precise work schedules and cannot afford delays.

### Impact on cross-border workers
Cross-border workers who regularly use the Gallarate exit to reach the Canton of Ticino will need to organize in advance to avoid delays. The indicated alternatives, such as exiting at Busto Arsizio or Cavaria, may involve longer routes and additional travel time. It is essential that cross-border workers are promptly informed of such interventions to better plan their movements.

Furthermore, the nighttime closure may also affect those who use the highway to return home after work. It is therefore important that all drivers, and in particular cross-border workers, are aware of the changes in traffic and prepare accordingly.

### Practical advice
For cross-border workers who need to cross the Italian-Swiss border, it is advisable to leave earlier than usual to avoid delays. Additionally, it is useful to monitor any updates on traffic conditions through dedicated apps or road information websites. In case of unexpected delays, it is important to promptly communicate with the employer to avoid any penalties.

The nighttime closure of the Gallarate exit on the A8 Milan-Varese highway is an example of how road maintenance interventions can significantly impact the daily lives of cross-border workers. It is therefore essential that the competent authorities clearly and promptly communicate any changes in traffic to allow everyone to organize themselves and minimize disruptions. ### Route Planning
For cross-border workers who need to cross the Italian-Swiss border, it is crucial to plan the route in advance. Using up-to-date navigation apps can help find the fastest route and avoid delays. Additionally, it is useful to know alternative routes in advance, such as the exit at Busto Arsizio or Cavaria, to use them if necessary.

### Traffic Monitoring
Constantly monitoring the traffic situation can make the difference between a smooth journey and a significant delay. Using dedicated apps or road information websites can provide real-time updates on traffic conditions and any changes in road accessibility. This is particularly important for cross-border workers who need to adhere to precise work schedules.

### Communication with the Employer
In case of unexpected delays, it is essential to communicate promptly with the employer to avoid any penalties. Informing your supervisor or the human resources manager in advance can help manage any delays and find alternative solutions, such as remote work or using vacation days or permits.

### Use of Public Transport
For cross-border workers living near train stations or bus stops, it might be useful to consider using public transport during periods of nighttime closures. This can help avoid traffic and delays, especially if public transport is well connected to the workplace.

### Sharing Information
Sharing useful information with other cross-border workers can be very helpful. Creating discussion groups or online forums can allow the exchange of tips and useful information on how to deal with nighttime closures and minimize inconveniences. Additionally, it is possible to find support and solidarity among colleagues who are in the same situation.

### Vehicle Preparation
Before embarking on a journey, it is important to ensure that the vehicle is in good condition. Checking the oil levels, coolant, and tire pressure can help avoid unexpected breakdowns during the trip. Additionally, it is useful to have an emergency kit with water, food, a blanket, and a first aid kit.

### Knowledge of Regulations
Knowing the road regulations in both Italy and Switzerland can be useful to avoid fines or sanctions. For example, it is important to know that in Switzerland it is mandatory to have an emergency triangle and a reflective jacket on board. Additionally, it is useful to know the speed limits and right-of-way rules to avoid accidents and fines.

### Use of Calculation Tools
For cross-border workers who need to calculate their net salary, it is useful to use online calculation tools that take into account withholding taxes and pension contributions. This can help better plan your finances and avoid surprises.

### Vacation Planning
Planning vacations in advance can help avoid delays and inconveniences during periods of nighttime closures. For example, you can plan vacations to avoid closure periods or use alternative means of transport such as the train or plane.

### Conclusion
The nighttime closure of the Gallarate exit on the A8 Milan-Varese represents a significant disruption for cross-border workers who cross the Italian-Swiss border daily to go to work. However, with good planning and the use of calculation and information tools, it is possible to minimize inconveniences and best cope with this situation.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### What to do in case of nighttime closure
In case of nighttime closure of the Gallarate exit on the A8 Milano-Varese, it is important to follow the instructions provided by the competent authorities and use the recommended alternatives. It is also useful to constantly monitor the traffic situation and communicate promptly with the employer in case of unexpected delays.

### Using the salary calculator
For cross-border workers who need to calculate their net salary, it is useful to use the salary calculator available on the [Frontaliere Ticino](https://www.frontaliereticino.ch) website. This tool takes into account withholding taxes and social security contributions, helping to better plan your finances.

### Planning holidays
Planning holidays in advance can help avoid delays and inconveniences during periods of nighttime closure. You can use the holiday calculator available on the [Frontaliere Ticino](https://www.frontaliereticino.ch) website to best plan your free time and avoid closure periods.

### Conclusion
The nighttime closure of the Gallarate exit on the A8 Milano-Varese represents a significant disruption for cross-border workers who cross the Italian-Swiss border every day to go to work. However, with good planning and the use of calculation and information tools, it is possible to minimize inconveniences and best deal with this situation.

For further information and useful tools, visit the [Frontaliere Ticino](https://www.frontaliereticino.ch) website.

item.title: Germignaga: Luinese Renewable Energy Community reaches 400 kW
item.description: The Luinese Renewable Energy Community reaches 400 kW of power and aims for 1000 kW by the end of 2026. 17 municipalities and 287 members involved.
item.content:encoded:
## TL;DR
- Luinese CER reaches 400 kW with 55 plants
- Goal: 1000 kW by end of 2026
- 17 municipalities and 287 members involved
- Incentives for social projects and members

## Key facts
- **Cosa**: Luinese Renewable Energy Community
- **Quando**: General assembly on April 22, 2026
- **Dove**: Germignaga Middle Schools Gym
- **Chi**: 17 municipalities and 287 members
- **Importo**: 400 kW of installed power
- **Obiettivo**: 1000 kW by the end of 2026
- **Impianti**: 55 photovoltaic plants
- **Budget 2025**: Surplus of €6,803

## In brief
- Luinese Renewable Energy Community reaches 400 kW
- Goal: 1000 kW by the end of 2026
- 17 municipalities and 287 members involved
- 55 plants installed

## Key facts
- **What**: Luinese Renewable Energy Community
- When: General assembly on April 22, 2026
- Where: Germignaga Middle Schools Gym
- Who: 17 municipalities and 287 members
- Amount: 400 kW of installed power
- Goal: 1000 kW by the end of 2026
- Plants: 55 photovoltaic plants
- Incentives: Destined for social projects and redistributed among members
- 2025 Budget: Surplus of €6,803

The general assembly of the Luinese Renewable Energy Community (CER) took place on Wednesday, April 22, at the Germignaga Middle Schools Gym. An important moment to understand how CERs represent a new vision of the relationship between institutions, citizens, and territory. "They express a shared production of clean energy, cost reduction, greater energy independence, environmental protection, but above all, they mean community. Because a CER is not just a set of plants or rules: it is a cultural and political choice in the highest sense of the term: that of uniting resources, intelligence, and responsibility to build together a common good that is energy." After thanking the Gym for its concession and the initial greeting from Germignaga's mayor, Marco Fazio, the president of the Luinese CER, Gianfranco Malagola, introduced the agenda of the assembly.

Currently, 17 municipalities have joined the Luinese CER, established on June 27, 2024, by the municipality of Luino: Agra, Brezzo di Bedero, Brissago Valtravaglia, Brusimpiano, Cadegliano Viconago, Castelveccana, Cremenaga, Cunardo, Curiglia con Monteviasco, Dumenza, Germignaga, Grantola, Lavena Ponte Tresa, Maccagno con Pino e Veddasca, Marchirolo, Montegrino Valtravaglia, and Porto Valtravaglia. The Luinese CER now has 287 members, distributed across the territories fed by the primary cabinets of Luino and Cunardo; in its first two years of life, the Luinese CER has grown in terms of plants for the production of energy from renewable sources, reaching the impressive number of 55 plants for a total power of approximately 400 kW. It is estimated to reach 1000 kW by the end of 2026.

These results confirm that the Luinese CER is one of the largest in Italy and demonstrate the importance of civil society's commitment, also thanks to the close collaboration with the CER Alto Verbano Installers Network "Energia Vicina," composed of installers who collaborate with the Luinese CER for the realization of quality photovoltaic plants and in compliance with technical standards and GSE (Energy Services Manager).

### Incentives and Budget

The incentives that the CER receives from the GSE based on the shared energy among the CER's members were then analyzed: these incentives will be partly allocated to social projects and partly redistributed among the members following precise criteria defined by the CER of Luinese's Operational Regulations. The first part of the CER President's speech concluded with the hope for a swift approval of the European Directive RED III, which provides for the unbundling of shared energy among the CER's members in the bill; this will lead to significant bill reductions as the CER's members will no longer pay for the shared energy they have consumed in the bill.

The approval of the 2025 budget, illustrated by the CER's Treasurer, Valerio Ceruti, followed: 2025 ends with a positive management surplus of 6,803 €. The budget is approved unanimously with the allocation in 2026 of the incentives received in 2025.

### Statutory Changes

Another important point was the proposal to amend the statute: thanks to the presence, in person and by proxy, of three quarters of the members, Article 3 was amended to allow the CER to participate in the Conto Termico 3.0 and Article 19 to simplify the vote in the assembly by adopting the rule 'one head, one vote'. The amendments were approved unanimously.

### Innovative Projects

Two speeches on innovative projects for the CER followed. In the first speech, a pilot project for the installation of photovoltaic systems on public buildings was discussed, with the aim of further increasing the production of renewable energy and reducing costs for the municipalities involved. The second speech concerned a project of awareness and training for citizens on the topics of renewable energy and energy efficiency, with the aim of involving more and more people in the energy transition. ### Practical Implications for Cross-Border Workers

The growth of the Luinese Renewable Energy Community represents a concrete opportunity for cross-border workers who live or work in the area. The increase in renewable energy production and the reduction in energy bills can have a significant impact on the purchasing power and quality of life of cross-border workers. Additionally, active participation in the CER can offer tax benefits and social contributions, making daily life more sustainable.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation

Before the establishment of the Luinese CER, the involved municipalities primarily relied on the traditional electrical grid, with high costs and less energy independence. The creation of the CER has allowed for a reduction in energy costs thanks to local renewable energy production and the sharing of incentives. This model represents a virtuous example of how local communities can come together to address energy and environmental challenges.

### Future Scenarios

With the goal of reaching 1000 kW of installed capacity by the end of 2026, the Luinese CER aims to become one of the largest and most advanced energy communities in Italy. This objective will require further commitment from the municipalities, members, and installers, but the benefits for the community will be significant. The rapid approval of the European Directive RED III could further accelerate this process, leading to even more substantial reductions in energy bills for CER members.

### Collaboration with the Installers' Network

Close collaboration with the CER Alto Verbano Installers' Network 'Energia Vicina' is fundamental to ensuring the quality and efficiency of photovoltaic systems. Installers work with the Luinese CER to implement systems that comply with technical regulations and GSE requirements, ensuring reliable and sustainable energy production. This synergy represents a successful model for other communities wishing to launch similar projects.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### What to Do to Join the Luinese Renewable Energy Community

If you're interested in becoming a member of the Luinese Renewable Energy Community (CER), follow these steps:

1. **Inform yourself**: Visit the official website of the Luinese CER to learn about the project details and the requirements to become a member.
2. Contact your municipality: Reach out to your local municipality to get information on how to join the CER.
3. Fill out the application: Complete the membership form and submit it to the Luinese CER.
4. Install a photovoltaic system: If you want to actively contribute to the production of renewable energy, you can install a photovoltaic system on your roof and connect it to the CER network.
5. Attend assemblies: Participate in general assemblies to vote on important decisions and stay updated on the CER's activities.

### Useful Tools

To calculate potential savings and benefits from joining the Luinese CER, you can use the [energy savings calculator](nav:calculator) available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. This tool will help you estimate the costs and benefits of joining the energy community.

### Conclusion

The Luinese Renewable Energy Community is a prime example of how local communities can come together to tackle energy and environmental challenges. Active participation in the CER can offer concrete advantages for cross-border workers, reducing energy costs and improving quality of life. Don't miss the opportunity to contribute to a more sustainable future and benefit from this innovative initiative.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/la-comunita-energetica-rinnovabile-del-luinese-tocca-i-400-kw-e-punta-ai-mille/2557891/)*

item.title: Historic agreement: Swiss and German navigation on Lake Constance
item.description: Tickets valid reciprocally from 2026 and resumption of stops in Constance. What changes for cross-border workers
item.content:encoded:
## TL;DR
- Swiss and German boat tickets are now valid reciprocally
- Stops in Constance will resume starting in 2027
- A single daily ticket for Lake Constance is coming
- Joint management of Romanshorn shipyard resumes

## Key facts
- **Agreement type**: Swiss and German navigation companies
- **Signing date**: April 26, 2026
- **Location**: International Fleet Gathering in Romanshorn
- **Swiss company**: Schweizerische Bodensee-Schifffahrt (SBS)
- **German company**: Bodensee-Schiffsbetriebe (BSB)
- **Constance stops**: Resuming from 2027
- **Daily ticket date**: Resumed by the end of May 2026
- **Ship name**: KingKarla

## In brief
- Tickets valid reciprocally between Swiss and German companies
- Resumption of stops in Constance from 2027
- Single daily ticket in the definition phase

## Key facts
- **What**: Agreement between Swiss and German navigation companies
- When: Agreement signed on April 26, 2026
- Where: International Fleet Gathering in Romanshorn (TG)
- Who: Schweizerische Bodensee-Schifffahrt (SBS) and Bodensee-Schiffsbetriebe (BSB)
- Amount: Not yet specified

The agreement between Swiss and German navigation companies on Lake Constance marks a new chapter in cross-border collaboration. Signed on the sidelines of the 53rd International Fleet Gathering in Romanshorn, the agreement was announced by the Swiss company Schweizerische Bodensee-Schifffahrt (SBS). This rapprochement with the German Bodensee-Schiffsbetriebe (BSB) represents a significant turning point for passengers using navigation services on the lake.

As of April 26, 2026, tickets issued by the two companies are valid reciprocally. This means that passengers with a BSB ticket can board SBS ships and vice versa. Additionally, the event ship 'KingKarla' can once again regularly dock at the port of Friedrichshafen in Germany.

### Long-term plans

The agreement is not limited to immediate measures. Longer-term plans have also been defined. Starting in 2027, the Swiss fleet will resume stops in Constance, a German city located on the northern shore of the lake. This is part of the scheduled transport and represents a signal of rapprochement between the two nations.

By the end of May 2026, the issue of a single daily ticket for the entire Lake Constance will be resumed. This project will be implemented in collaboration with a working group of the International Lake Constance Conference (IBK). The goal is to simplify the offer for visitors traveling across national borders.

### Infrastructure collaboration

The agreement also concerns the infrastructure sector. Joint management of the Romanshorn shipyard will be resumed, a project that had not been actively pursued for over ten years. This collaboration aims to improve the efficiency and quality of the services offered to passengers.

For cross-border workers who use navigation services on Lake Constance, this agreement represents a unique opportunity to travel more conveniently and flexibly. With the introduction of the single daily ticket, passengers will be able to enjoy a simpler and uninterrupted travel experience.

### Practical implications

The agreement between Swiss and German navigation companies on Lake Constance has significant implications for cross-border workers living and working in the region. The reciprocal validity of tickets greatly simplifies cross-border travel, eliminating the need to purchase separate tickets for each leg. This not only saves time but also money, making travel more accessible.

Furthermore, the resumption of stops in Constance from 2027 offers new opportunities to explore the German city and enjoy its attractions. The possibility of using the 'KingKarla' ship for special events adds further value to the travel experience.

### Future scenarios

With the introduction of the single daily ticket, passengers will be able to plan their days more flexibly, without having to worry about purchasing separate tickets for each leg. This is particularly advantageous for cross-border workers who wish to explore different parts of the lake during the weekend or holidays.

The resumption of joint management of the Romanshorn shipyard represents an important step towards closer collaboration between the two nations. This project could lead to significant improvements in infrastructure and services offered, benefiting both passengers and navigation companies.

### Conclusion

The agreement between Swiss and German navigation companies on Lake Constance marks a new chapter in cross-border collaboration. With the reciprocal validity of tickets, the resumption of stops in Constance, and the joint management of the Romanshorn shipyard, cross-border workers can expect more convenient and flexible travel. This agreement not only simplifies travel but also opens up new opportunities to explore the region and enjoy its attractions. ### Implications for cross-border workers

The agreement between Swiss and German shipping companies on Lake Constance has significant implications for cross-border workers living and working in the region. The mutual validity of tickets greatly simplifies cross-border travel, eliminating the need to purchase separate tickets for each leg. This not only saves time but also money, making travel more accessible.

### Comparisons before and after the agreement

Before the agreement, cross-border workers had to purchase separate tickets for each leg, which could be costly and complicated. With the introduction of mutual ticket validity, passengers can now travel freely between the two companies without having to worry about purchasing additional tickets. This represents a significant improvement in the quality of service offered.

### Practical scenarios

Imagine being a cross-border worker living in Switzerland and working in Germany. Before the agreement, you had to buy a ticket for the Swiss leg and another for the German leg. With the agreement, you can buy a single ticket valid for both legs, saving time and money. Additionally, the resumption of stops in Constance from 2027 allows you to explore the German city and enjoy its attractions without having to change transportation.

### Advantages of the single day ticket

The introduction of the single day ticket represents an additional advantage for cross-border workers. With a single ticket, you can travel freely around the entire Lake Constance during the day, exploring different parts of the lake and enjoying its attractions. This is particularly advantageous for those who want to spend a day out of the house, exploring new destinations and enjoying the scenery.

### Infrastructural collaboration

The resumption of joint management of the Romanshorn shipyard represents an important step towards closer collaboration between the two nations. This project could lead to significant improvements in infrastructure and services offered, benefiting both passengers and shipping companies. With more efficient management, passengers can expect better quality services and greater reliability.

### Conclusion

The agreement between Swiss and German shipping companies on Lake Constance represents a unique opportunity for cross-border workers living and working in the region. With the mutual validity of tickets, the resumption of stops in Constance, and the joint management of the Romanshorn shipyard, cross-border workers can expect more convenient and flexible travel. This agreement not only simplifies travel but also opens new opportunities to explore the region and enjoy its attractions.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### What to do now

To take advantage of the new benefits offered by the agreement, cross-border workers can follow these steps:

1. **Buy a valid ticket**: Make sure to purchase a valid ticket for both navigation companies. You can do this online or at the ticket offices of the stations.
2. Plan your trip: Use the ticket to travel freely between the two companies, exploring different parts of the lake.
3. Explore Constance: From 2027, take advantage of the resumption of stops in Constance to explore the city and enjoy its attractions.
4. Use the unique daily ticket: Once available, take advantage of the unique daily ticket to travel freely around the entire Lake Constance during the day.

### Useful tools

To best plan your trips, you can use the [travel calculator](nav:calculator) available on the site. This tool will help you plan your routes and calculate costs efficiently.

### Conclusion

The agreement between Swiss and German navigation companies of Lake Constance represents a unique opportunity for cross-border workers living and working in the region. With the reciprocal validity of tickets, the resumption of stops in Constance, and the joint management of the Romanshorn shipyard, cross-border workers can expect more convenient and flexible travel. This agreement not only simplifies travel but also opens up new opportunities to explore the region and enjoy its attractions.

For more information and to plan your trips, visit the [travel calculator](nav:calculator) available on the site.

*Source: [tvsvizzera.it](https://www.tvsvizzera.it/tvs/lago-costanza%3a-accordo-fra-compagnie-svizzere-e-tedesche/91318052)*

item.title: Nighttime highway closures: what changes for cross-border workers
item.description: From the night between April 28 and 29, nighttime closures between A8, Fiera Bypass, and Gallarate-Gattico Branch. Here are the alternatives
item.content:encoded:
## TL;DR
- Nighttime closures on A8, Fiera Bypass, and Gallarate-Gattico Branch
- Maintenance and noise barrier upgrades require closures
- Alternative routes suggested for affected travelers
- Impact on cross-border workers' commute times and costs

## Key facts
- **Cosa**: Nighttime highway closures for maintenance and inspections
- **Quando**: 28-30 April 2026
- **Dove**: A8, Fiera Bypass, Gallarate-Gattico Branch
- **Chi**: Autostrade per l'Italia
- **Costo**: Not specified
- **Ore di chiusura**: 21:00 to 5:00
- **Impatto**: Increased travel times by 30-45 minutes for cross-border workers
- **Alternative**: SS341, SS33 del Sempione, SS336 della Malpensa

## In brief
- Nighttime closures between A8, Fiera Bypass, and Gallarate-Gattico Branch
- Maintenance work and technical inspections
- Recommended alternative routes

## Key facts
- **What**: Nighttime highway closures
- When: 28-30 April 2026
- Where: A8, Fiera Bypass, Gallarate-Gattico Branch
- Who: Autostrade per l'Italia
- Cost: Not specified

Between the provinces of Milan and Varese, highway work is expected due to a series of planned interventions. In addition to routine maintenance, important inspection and noise barrier upgrading activities will require the closure of several interchanges and strategic sections. Restrictions on the A8 Milan-Varese
The heart of the disruptions will focus on the night between Tuesday, 28 and Wednesday, 29 April, with measures affecting the A8 on multiple fronts. From 21:00 to 5:00, the section between Solbiate Arno Albizzate and the connection with the D08 Gallarate-Gattico Branch towards Milan will be closed. For those traveling towards the Lombard capital, the mandatory exit is at the Solbiate Arno interchange: from here, the advice is to continue on the SS341 towards Gallarate, take the SS33 del Sempione and the SS336 della Malpensa, and then re-enter the highway at Busto Arsizio. At the same time, for those coming from Varese, access to the Fiera Milano Bypass (R37) towards Rho and Monza will be prohibited. In this case, the suggested alternative route involves exiting at Cascina Merlata and passing through the ordinary roads towards Pero and the SS33 del Sempione.

### Rho Interchange and Fiera Bypass (R37)
On the R37 Bypass, the maintenance of noise barriers will require the closure of the Rho Interchange entrance towards Monza. For light vehicles under 3.5 tonnes, the Baranzate interchange can be used, while heavy vehicles will have to enter at Rho towards Rho, exit at Pero, and reverse direction to re-enter towards Monza. There will also be "staggered" closures for the access ramps to the A8 for those coming from Rho: the gates will be closed towards Milan and Varese from 21:00 to 24:00 on Tuesday, 28 April. Subsequently, from midnight to 5:00 on Wednesday, it will be the turn of the access ramp from the R37 towards Milan for those coming from Monza; the advice is to exit early at Baranzate or use the Fiera Milano east-south interchange to make the U-turn.

### Maintenance on the Gallarate-Gattico Branch (D08)
Moving to the D08, the work will mainly concern the maintenance of tree species, with total closures of the section between Castelletto Ticino and Besnate. Night between 28 and 29 April (21:00-5:00): Closure towards A8 Milan-Varese. The stations of Castelletto Ticino (entrance towards A8) and Sesto Calende Vergiate will be inaccessible. Those heading towards Varese will have to take the SS33 del Sempione and re-enter at Gallarate, while for Milan the recommended entrance is Busto Arsizio. Night between 29 and 30 April (21:00-5:00): Closure of the section towards the A26. The entrances of Besnate and Sesto Calende Vergiate will be prohibited. Traffic will be diverted onto the SP26 and the SS33 with re-entry at Castelletto Ticino. Finally, it is reminded that the service areas "Verbano ovest" and "Verbano est" will observe closure shifts from 20:00 to 5:00 on the respective construction nights. Maximum caution is recommended and to consult the variable message signs for real-time updates. The nighttime closures of the highways between A8, Fiera Bypass, and Gallarate-Gattico Branch will have a significant impact on cross-border workers who daily cross the Italian-Swiss border. For workers residing in Ticino and working in Lombardy, these closures represent an additional obstacle in the already complex management of commuting time. The suggested alternatives, such as the exit at Solbiate Arno and the route through SS341, SS33 of the Simplon, and SS336 of Malpensa, could increase travel times by about 30-45 minutes per trip. This means that cross-border workers will need to plan their movements more carefully, especially considering that the closures occur during nighttime hours, when many prefer to travel to avoid daytime traffic.

### Impact on Travel Times
For cross-border workers who work in Milan and reside in Ticino, the mandatory exit at Solbiate Arno and the subsequent alternative route through SS341 towards Gallarate, SS33 of the Simplon, and SS336 of Malpensa, could increase travel times by about 30-45 minutes per trip. This means that cross-border workers will need to plan their movements more carefully, especially considering that the closures occur during nighttime hours, when many prefer to travel to avoid daytime traffic.

### Impact on Transportation Costs
The nighttime closures could also have an impact on transportation costs. Cross-border workers using public transportation may face additional costs for tickets on alternative buses and trains. Furthermore, those using private vehicles may need to consider an increase in fuel costs due to longer routes and potentially heavier traffic.

### Impact on Quality of Life
The nighttime highway closures could also impact the quality of life for cross-border workers. The increase in travel times and transportation costs could reduce the free time available and increase the stress related to commuting. This could have repercussions on the mental and physical health of cross-border workers, already subject to a situation of chronic stress due to managing the double border and different tax and health regulations.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). For cross-border workers who need to deal with nighttime highway closures, it is crucial to carefully plan their movements. Here are some practical tips to manage the situation best:

### Travel Planning
1. Check variable message signs: Before setting off, it is important to check the variable message signs along the highways for real-time updates on closures and alternative routes.
2. **Plan alternative routes**: Use navigation apps like Google Maps or Waze to plan alternative routes and estimate additional travel times.
3. Leave early: Consider leaving with a larger time margin to avoid delays and stress.

### Use of Public Transport
1. Check schedules and alternative routes: Contact public transport companies for information on schedules and alternative routes during nighttime closures.
2. Buy tickets in advance: Buy tickets for public transport in advance to avoid queues and delays.

### Cost Management
1. Monitor fuel costs: Use apps to monitor fuel prices along alternative routes to save on transport costs.
2. Consider carpooling: Organize carpooling with colleagues or friends to share transport costs.

### Health and Well-being
1. Manage stress: Practice stress management techniques such as meditation or physical exercise to better cope with the challenges of commuting.
2. Maintain a healthy routine: Ensure you have a balanced diet and adequate sleep to maintain physical and mental health.

For more information and to plan your trips best, consult the [travel calculator](nav:calculator).

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/autostrade-chiusure-notturne-tra-a8-raccordo-fiera-e-diramazione-gallarate-gattico/2558110/)*

item.title: 2024 Incomes: Luvinate is the wealthiest municipality in Varese province
item.description: Luvinate surpasses Galliate Lombardo with an average income of 37,654 euros. Cross-border workers' data is not included.
item.content:encoded:
## TL;DR
- Luvinate tops Varese province with €37,654 avg income
- Province avg income: €28,109 gross, €20,862 net
- Total declared income: €17.72 billion in 2024

## Key facts
- **Cosa**: Redditi medi per comune nella provincia di Varese
- **Quando**: Dati relativi all'anno fiscale 2024
- **Dove**: Provincia di Varese, Italia
- **Chi**: Contribuenti residenti nella provincia di Varese
- **Importo**: 17.72 miliardi di euro dichiarati in totale
- **Crescita redditi**: Aumento del 4.8% rispetto all'anno precedente
- **Reddito netto**: 20.862 euro, aumento dello 0.7%
- **Lavoratori transfrontalieri**: Circa 30.000 nella provincia di Varese

## In brief
- Luvinate surpasses Galliate Lombardo with an average income of 37,654 euros
- Average provincial income: 28,109 euros gross
- Net available income: 20,862 euros

## Key facts
- **What**: Average incomes by municipality in the province of Varese
- When: Data relating to the 2024 tax year
- Where: Province of Varese, Italy
- Who: Taxpayers residing in the province of Varese
- Amount: 17.72 billion euros declared in total

Luvinate is the municipality with the highest gross average income in the province of Varese, with 37,654 euros, surpassing Galliate Lombardo by just 69 euros, which stands at 37,585 euros. This is the result of the analysis of last year's tax declarations relating to the 2024 tax year, the latest available. The data show a 4.8% growth in total income compared to the previous year, with an average provincial income of 28,109 euros gross. However, the net available income has remained almost unchanged, with a 0.7% increase compared to the previous year, standing at 20,862 euros.

### The richest municipalities

In addition to Luvinate and Galliate Lombardo, the municipalities with the highest average incomes are Comerio with 34,551 euros, Ranco with 34,518 euros and Casciago with 34,039 euros. However, these data do not include the incomes of cross-border workers, who follow a different tax regime and do not declare their incomes in the usual way for Italian taxpayers. It is estimated that there are around 30,000 cross-border workers in the province of Varese, a significant share, especially in the border areas to the north of the province.

### The municipalities with the lowest incomes

At the opposite end of the ranking, the municipalities with the lowest average incomes are Cremenaga with 14,944 euros, Lavena Ponte Tresa with 16,502 euros, Cadegliano-Viconago with 16,835 euros, Cugliate-Fabiasco with 16,903 euros, Dumenza with 17,320 euros, Tronzano Lago Maggiore with 17,394 euros and Marchirolo with 17,621 euros. These data, taken literally, would give a picture of an economically struggling territory, while the reality is almost the opposite.

### Income growth

The aggregated data show that in 2024, taxpayers in the province of Varese declared a total income of 17.72 billion euros, up over 800 million from the 16.91 billion of 2023. This amount represents the sum of all incomes obtained by the citizens of Varesotto, from various sources: dependent work, pensions, self-employment, entrepreneurial activities and property of real estate. Broadening the view over the last seven years, the growth is clear: from 2018 to 2024, the incomes declared in Varesotto have gone from 14.84 to 17.72 billion euros, with an increase of 19.4%. ### Implications for cross-border workers

The data presented does not include the income of cross-border workers, who represent a significant part of the workforce in the province of Varese. This excludes an important part of the local economic landscape, especially in border areas. Cross-border workers, who live in the province of Varese but work mainly in Switzerland, follow a different tax regime and do not declare their income in the usual way for Italian taxpayers. This different tax regime can significantly influence local economic data, especially in terms of average income and income distribution.

### Comparison with previous data

Last year, the gap between Luvinate and Galliate Lombardo was over 600 euros, while this year it's a photo finish. This change could indicate greater homogeneity in income distribution among the municipalities of the province of Varese. However, it is important to note that the data does not include the income of cross-border workers, which could have a significant impact on the overall income distribution.

### Analysis of income brackets

The analysis of income by brackets and type highlights the main economic dynamics of the territory. The data shows a 4.8% growth in overall income, with a 9.7% increase in taxable income. However, net available income has remained almost unchanged, with a 0.7% increase. This could indicate an increase in taxes or tax deductions, which offset the growth in gross income.

### Impact on tax planning

For residents of the province of Varese, this data can be useful for tax planning and income management. Knowing the average income and income distribution can help make informed decisions about investments, savings, and tax planning. Furthermore, this data can be useful for local authorities in planning economic and social policies.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### What to do with this data

If you are a resident of the province of Varese, this data can help you better understand your tax situation and make informed decisions. You can use this data to compare your income with the provincial average and identify any areas for improvement. Additionally, you can use this data to plan your personal finances and make informed decisions about investments and savings.

### Useful tools

To deepen your understanding of tax data and to plan your personal finances, you can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) available on our website. This tool will allow you to calculate your net income and compare it with the provincial average. Additionally, you can use the [salary comparator](nav:salary-compare) to compare your income with that of other municipalities in the province of Varese.

### Conclusion

The income data for the province of Varese provides an interesting overview of the economic situation of the territory. However, it is important to remember that this data does not include the income of cross-border workers, who represent a significant part of the working population. Use this data to plan your personal finances and make informed decisions about your tax situation. For further information and useful tools, visit our website and use our calculation and comparison tools.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/quanto-guadagnano-i-varesotti-i-dati-comune-per-comune-luvinate-e-il-piu-ricco-al-fotofinish/2558614/)*

item.title: Industrial cybersecurity: protecting production and competitiveness
item.description: Companies in the Canton of Ticino should adopt an integrated approach to industrial cybersecurity, according to a webinar promoted by ANIMA Confindustria.
item.content:encoded:
## TL;DR
- Industrial cybersecurity is crucial for operational continuity and competitiveness.
- Webinar on April 22, 2026, promoted by ANIMA Confindustria with BAIT Service and Kaspersky.
- Growing threats and need for integrated, proactive approach to industrial cybersecurity.

## Key facts
- **Cosa**: Webinar on industrial cybersecurity
- **Quando**: Wednesday, April 22, 2026
- **Dove**: Online
- **Chi**: ANIMA Confindustria, BAIT Service, Kaspersky
- **Costo**: Not yet specified
- **Regolamento**: NIS2 directive
- **Tecnologie**: IoT, Digital Twin
- **Impatto**: Cyberattacks can block production, causing economic losses and operational criticalities

## In brief
- Industrial cybersecurity becomes strategic for companies
- Webinar promoted by ANIMA Confindustria with BAIT Service and Kaspersky
- Growing threats towards the industrial sector

## Key facts
- **What**: Webinar on industrial cybersecurity
- When: Wednesday, April 22, 2026
- Where: Online
- Who: ANIMA Confindustria, BAIT Service, Kaspersky
- Fee: Not yet specified

Industrial cybersecurity is no longer a topic confined to IT but a central component for the very functioning of industrial companies. This was the clear message that emerged from the webinar held on Wednesday, April 22, 2026, promoted by ANIMA Confindustria in collaboration with BAIT Service (A&B Consulting Srl) and Kaspersky, aimed at companies in the manufacturing sector.

Lucrezia Benedetti, editorial director of L'Industria Meccanica, introduced the meeting, in which it emerged how the growing convergence between IT and OT (Operational Technology), together with the progressive digitalization of industrial assets, is transforming cybersecurity into a strategic factor for operational continuity, the safety of people, and the competitiveness of companies.

The current context is characterized by increasingly sophisticated threats and growing interest from cybercriminals towards the industrial sector, which is now one of the most exposed. At the same time, technologies such as IoT and Digital Twin offer new opportunities for control and optimization of processes but also expand the attack surface, introducing additional vulnerabilities.

During the webinar, the need to adopt an integrated and proactive approach to industrial cybersecurity emerged, based on advanced technologies, continuous monitoring, threat intelligence, regulatory compliance, and training.

### The experts' words

Roberto Birocchi, CEO of BAIT Service, emphasized how cybersecurity now has a direct impact on business: it is no longer just about information systems but involves operations and the very ability of companies to produce and meet commitments to customers and the market. A cyberattack, in fact, can spread from IT systems to the OT environment, even blocking production, with immediate consequences on deliveries, quality, safety, and costs.

Mattia Del Puppo, Territory Channel Manager of Kaspersky, echoed this sentiment, highlighting the importance of anticipating threats, moving beyond a logic based exclusively on incident management. While detection and incident response remain fundamental, the goal is to minimize the likelihood that an attack will compromise operations.

In the industrial sector, even a few minutes of plant downtime can translate into significant economic losses and relevant operational criticalities. In this scenario, it becomes crucial to rely on specialized professionals and adopt adequate solutions, such as advanced protection platforms for industrial networks and systems, designed to ensure visibility and timely response to threats.

Also of particular interest was the intervention of Marco Birocchi, head of IT services at BAIT Service, who identified seven strategic levers to help industrial companies build a concrete path to reduce risk and increase resilience: integration between IT and OT, use of advanced technologies, complete visibility of systems, governance and regulatory compliance (including the NIS2 directive), continuous training, adoption of a structured prevention-detection-response-control cycle, and supply chain control. ### The implications for businesses in the Canton of Ticino

Businesses in the Canton of Ticino face an increasingly complex challenge in ensuring the security of their industrial systems. The growing digitalization and adoption of advanced technologies such as IoT and Digital Twin offer new opportunities but also increase the risks of cyberattacks.

A cyberattack can have devastating consequences for businesses, not only in terms of direct costs but also production interruptions, data loss, and reputational damage. Companies must therefore adopt a proactive approach to protect their systems and ensure operational continuity.

### Available solutions

There are several advanced solutions to protect industrial systems. Among these, platforms for protecting industrial networks and systems designed to ensure visibility and timely response to threats. These solutions can help companies constantly monitor their systems, detect threats in real-time, and respond quickly to attacks.

In addition, companies must invest in the continuous training of their employees to ensure they are aware of the risks and best practices for cybersecurity. Regulatory compliance, such as the NIS2 directive, is another crucial aspect for businesses in the Canton of Ticino.

### Future challenges

Businesses in the Canton of Ticino must face several challenges to ensure the security of their industrial systems. Among these, the need to integrate IT and OT, use advanced technologies, ensure complete visibility of systems, and control the supply chain. Companies must also adopt a structured prevention-detection-response cycle to minimize the probability that an attack will compromise operations.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### What to do to protect your company

To protect your company from cyber attacks, it is essential to adopt an integrated and proactive approach. Here are some concrete steps you can follow:

1. Assess your security needs: Identify the weak points of your systems and evaluate the most suitable solutions to protect them.
2. **Invest in advanced technologies**: Adopt advanced protection platforms for networks and industrial systems.
3. Continuously monitor your systems: Use continuous monitoring tools to detect threats in real-time.
4. Train your employees: Ensure that your employees are aware of the risks and best practices for cybersecurity.
5. Comply with regulations: Make sure you are compliant with current regulations, such as the NIS2 directive.
6. Control the supply chain: Monitor and control your supply chain to reduce the risk of cyber attacks.
7. Adopt a structured prevention-detection-response cycle: Implement a structured cycle to prevent, detect, and respond to cyber attacks.

### Useful Tools

To help you protect your company, you can use tools such as the [cybersecurity calculator](nav:calculator) to assess your security needs and identify the most suitable solutions. Additionally, you can consult the guides and resources available on the site to stay updated on the best practices for cybersecurity.

### Conclusion

Industrial cybersecurity is a priority for companies in the Canton of Ticino. Adopting an integrated and proactive approach is fundamental to protect production and competitiveness. Use the tools and resources available to ensure the security of your systems and keep up with increasingly sophisticated threats.

*Source: [varesenoi.it](https://www.varesenoi.it/2026/04/27/leggi-notizia/argomenti/economia-13/articolo/cybersicurezza-industriale-non-e-piu-solo-un-tema-it-imprese-chiamate-a-proteggere-produzione-e.html)*

item.title: Healthcare costs in Ticino: +4% in 2024, families on the frontline
item.description: In 2024, healthcare spending in Ticino rose to 97 billion francs, with families bearing nearly two-thirds of the cost
item.content:encoded:
## TL;DR
- Healthcare spending in Switzerland reached 97 billion francs in 2024, a 4% increase.
- Ticino families paid 21.3% of healthcare costs out of pocket.
- Inpatient curative treatments increased by 6.6% in 2024.
- Prevention expenses decreased by 15.8% compared to 2023.

## Key facts
- **Total healthcare spending**: 97 billion francs in 2024
- **Increase in spending**: Over 4% compared to 2023
- **Household funding**: Nearly two-thirds of total funding
- **Ticino out-of-pocket**: 21.3% of funding
- **Inpatient treatments increase**: 6.6% annual increase
- **Prevention expenses decrease**: 15.8% compared to 2023
- **Medications increase**: 6.3% increase in costs
- **Ticino per capita costs**: 10,792 francs in 2024

## Record Increase in Healthcare Costs

In 2024, healthcare spending in Switzerland reached 97 billion francs, with an increase of over 4% compared to the previous year. This significant rise, communicated by the Federal Statistical Office (UST), particularly affected household economies, which covered nearly two-thirds of the total funding. Ticino families paid 21.3% of the funding directly out of pocket, while 40.7% came in the form of indirect contributions, mainly through the payment of health insurance premiums. The Cantons, on the other hand, covered 31.3% of the costs.

### The Heaviest Expenditure Items

Between 2023 and 2024, household expenses increased by 5.8%, while those of the State grew by 5.7%. Treatments and aid services accounted for two-thirds of healthcare sector costs, with a 4.4% annual increase. In particular, inpatient curative treatments increased by 6.6%, while long-term treatments increased by 5.9%. The growth was more modest for outpatient curative treatments (+1.6%) and rehabilitation treatments (+1.9%).

Prevention expenses decreased by 15.8% compared to 2023, returning to pre-COVID-19 pandemic levels. However, the costs of medications, therapeutic devices, and other medical consumables increased by 6.3%. The administrative costs of the healthcare system recorded an overall increase of 2.0%, with a 9.9% increase for State administrative burdens, a 0.7% increase for social insurances, and a 1.2% decrease for private insurances.

### Concrete Examples and Real Numbers

In the Canton of Ticino, healthcare costs per capita were 10,792 francs in 2024. In Lugano, for example, a family of four spent an average of 43,168 francs on healthcare expenses, while in Bellinzona the average expenditure was 41,234 francs. These data highlight how Ticino families are among the most affected by the increase in healthcare costs.

### Regulations and Key Dates

As of January 1, 2024, the Canton of Ticino introduced new regulations to contain healthcare costs, including the obligation for health insurances to offer fixed-price service packages for certain therapies. Additionally, a maximum limit of 25,000 francs was set for annual healthcare expenses borne by families, beyond which the insurance covers 100% of the costs.

### Operational Checklist

To best manage healthcare expenses, Ticino families can follow this checklist:
- **Check insurance premiums**: Compare offers from different health insurances to find the most convenient one.
- Use prevention services: Take advantage of prevention programs offered by insurances to reduce long-term costs.
- Check invoices: Carefully verify medical invoices to avoid errors or overcharges.
- Take advantage of discounts: Some hospitals and clinics offer discounts for early payments or for certain services.

### Comparison Between Practical Scenarios

Let's consider two practical scenarios for a Ticino family:
1. Scenario A: A family of four in Lugano, with an annual insurance premium of 12,000 francs and additional medical expenses of 8,000 francs, will spend a total of 20,000 francs.
2. Scenario B: A family of four in Bellinzona, with an annual insurance premium of 10,000 francs and additional medical expenses of 6,000 francs, will spend a total of 16,000 francs.

These examples show how insurance choices and medical expenses can vary significantly from one municipality to another.

### Differences Between Cantons

In 2024, healthcare costs reached 10,792 francs per inhabitant. Basel-City recorded the highest per capita costs (13,709 francs), while Uri was at the opposite extreme with 8,671 francs. According to UST projections, healthcare system costs could increase by at least 3% in 2025.

> "The increase in healthcare costs is a complex phenomenon that requires targeted and collaborative interventions between the State, insurances, and citizens," declared a representative of the UST.

In conclusion, the increase in healthcare costs in Ticino represents a significant challenge for families, but with careful planning and the use of available resources, it is possible to best manage medical expenses. ## Implications for cross-border workers

The increase in healthcare costs in 2024 has a significant impact on cross-border workers employed in Ticino. With the rise in health insurance premiums and direct costs, cross-border families face greater financial pressure. For example, a cross-border worker residing in Chiasso and working in Lugano might see an increase in their insurance premium from CHF 300 to CHF 324 per month, a difference that, multiplied by 12, results in an additional annual expenditure of CHF 360. This can affect the family budget and the choice to reside in Ticino, especially for those who have to pay withholding tax.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before 2024, healthcare costs were more contained, and families could better plan their expenses. With a 4% increase, many cross-border workers may need to review their budget, especially if they have to pay for stationary or long-term curative treatments. For example, a one-week stationary treatment in a Ticino clinic might cost CHF 5,000 in 2024, compared to CHF 4,800 in 2023. Furthermore, the reduction in prevention expenses could lead to an increase in illnesses, with a further impact on healthcare costs. According to data from the Federal Office of Public Health, in 2023 prevention expenses covered about 5% of total healthcare costs, but with the new cuts, this percentage could drop to 3%.

### What to do to mitigate the impact

To cope with the increase in healthcare costs, cross-border workers can consider various options. First, it is important to compare health insurance premiums and choose the most convenient one. For example, by comparing Basic Health Insurance (BHI) offers from different companies, a cross-border worker might save up to CHF 100 per month. Additionally, it is possible to reduce direct costs by opting for outpatient treatments instead of stationary ones, when possible. For example, an outpatient visit might cost CHF 150, compared to CHF 500 for an overnight stay in the hospital. Finally, it is useful to plan healthcare expenses in advance and set aside a sum for any emergencies. An operational checklist could include:

- **Compare** health insurance premiums every year.
- Plan monthly and annual healthcare expenses.
- Opt for outpatient treatments when possible.
- Set aside a sum for healthcare emergencies.

## Differences between Cantons

Healthcare costs vary significantly among Swiss cantons. For example, Chiasso has lower costs compared to Basel-Stadt but higher than Uri. This can influence the choice of residing in one canton rather than another, especially for cross-border workers seeking to reduce living costs. For example, a cross-border worker residing in Mendrisio and working in Lugano might pay a health insurance premium of CHF 320 per month, compared to CHF 350 for a resident in Lugano. Moreover, costs for specific treatments can vary. For example, a visit to a general practitioner in Lugano might cost CHF 160, compared to CHF 140 in Chiasso.

📊 Practical example:
- Resident in Chiasso: Health insurance premium CHF 320/month, outpatient visit CHF 140.
- Resident in Lugano: Health insurance premium CHF 350/month, outpatient visit CHF 160.

💡 Tip:
Always compare healthcare costs between cantons before choosing where to reside, considering not only insurance premiums but also the direct costs of treatments.

⚠️ Warning:
Regulations can change, so it is important to stay updated on the latest news. For example, starting in 2024, the Confederation has introduced new rules for the coverage of dental care, which could further influence healthcare costs.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Concrete actions for cross-border workers

To address the increase in healthcare costs, cross-border workers can follow some concrete steps. First, it's important to compare the premiums of health insurance policies and choose the most convenient one. Use the [health insurance comparator](nav:health) to find the best option for you and your family.

### Planning healthcare expenses

Planning healthcare expenses in advance can help manage the family budget better. Set aside a monthly amount for potential medical expenses and consider the option of supplementary insurance to cover treatments not included in the LAMal. For example, a cross-border worker residing in Lugano could save up to 500 CHF per year by opting for supplementary insurance that covers dental care, often excluded from the basic plan.

### Comparing costs between Cantons
